author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew XX. 17-19.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And Jesus going up to Jerusalem took the twelve disciples apart in the way, and said unto them, Behold, we go up to Jerusalem; and the Son of Man shall be betrayed unto the chief priests and unto the Scribes, and they shall condemn Him to death, and shall deliver Him to the Gentiles to mock, and to scourge, and to crucify Him; and the third day He shall be raised.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He goeth not up at once to Jerusalem when He is come out of Galilee, but having first wrought miracles, and having stopped the mouths of Pharisees, and having discoursed with His disciples of renouncing possessions: for, “if thou wilt be perfect,” saith He, “sell that thou hast:”and of virginity, “He that is able to receive, let him receive it:”and of humility, “For except ye be converted, and become as little children, ye shall not enter into the kingdom of Heaven:”and of a recompense of the things here, “For whoso hath forsaken houses, or brethren, or sisters, shall receive an hundredfold in this world:”and of rewards there, “For he shall also inherit,” it is said, “eternal life:” then He assails the city next, and being on the point of going up, discourses again of His passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since it was likely that they, because they were not willing this should come to pass, would forget it, He is continually putting them in remembrance, exercising their mind by the frequency with which He reminded them, and diminishing their pain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He speaks with them “apart,” necessarily; for it was not meet that His discourse about these things should be published to the many; neither that it should be spoken plainly, for no advantage arose from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if the disciples were confounded at hearing these things, much more the multitude of the people.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then? was it not told to the people? you may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It was indeed told to the people also, but not so plainly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Destroy,” saith He, “this Temple, and in three days I will raise it up;”and, “This generation seeketh after a sign, and there shall no sign be given it, but the sign of Jonas;”and again, “Yet a little while am I with you, and ye shall seek me, and shall not find me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to the disciples not so, but as the other things He spake unto them more plainly, so also spake He this too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And for what purpose, if the multitude understood not the force of His sayings, were they spoken at all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That they might learn after these things, that fore-knowing it, He came to His passion, and willing it; not in ignorance, nor by constraint.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But to the disciples not for this cause only did He foretell it; but, as I have said, in order that having been exercised by the expectation, they might more easily endure the passion, and that it might not confound them by coming upon them without preparation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So for this cause, while at the beginning He spake of His death only, when they were practised and trained to hear of it, He adds the other circumstances also; as, for instance, that they should deliver Him to the Gentiles, that they should mock and scourge Him; as well on this account, as in order that when they saw the mournful events come to pass, they might expect from this the resurrection also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He who had not cloaked from them what would give pain, and what seemed to be matter of reproach, would reasonably be believed about good things too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But mark, I pray thee, how with regard to the time also He orders the thing wisely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither at the beginning did He tell them, lest He should disquiet them, neither at the time itself, lest by this again He should confound them; but when they had received sufficient proof of His power, when He had given them promises that were very great concerning life everlasting, then He introduces also what He had to say concerning these things, once and twice and often interweaving it with His miracles and His instructions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But another evangelist saith, that He brought in the prophets also as witnesses;and another again saith, that even they themselves understood not His words, but the saying was hid from them, and that they were amazed as they followed Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Surely then, one may say, the benefit of the prediction is taken away.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if they knew not what they were hearing, neither could they look for the event, and not looking for it, neither could they be exercised by their expectations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I say another thing also more perplexing than this: If they did not know, how were they sorry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For another saith, they were sorry.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If therefore they knew it not, how were they sorry?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How did Peter say, “Be it far from Thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This shall not be unto Thee?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then may we say?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That He should die indeed they knew, albeit they knew not clearly the mystery of the Incarnation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither did they know clearly about the resurrection, neither what He was to achieve; and this was hid from them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause also they felt pain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For some they had known to have been raised again by other persons, but for any one to have raised up himself again, and in such wise to have raised himself as not to die any more, they had never known.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This then they understood not, though often said; nay nor of this self-same death did they clearly know what it was, and how it should come on Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also they were amazed as they followed Him, but not for this cause only; but to me at least He seems even to amaze them by discoursing of His passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet none of these things made them take courage, and this when they were continually hearing about His resurrection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For together with His death this also especially troubled them, to hear that men should “mock and scourge Him,” and the like.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they considered His miracles, the possessed persons whom He had delivered, the dead whom He had raised, all the other marvellous works which He was doing, and then heard these things, they were amazed, if He who doeth these works is thus to suffer.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore they fell even into perplexity, and now believed, now disbelieved, and could not understand His sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So far at least were they from understanding clearly what He said, that the sons of Zebedee at the same time came to Him, and spake to Him of precedence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We desire,” it is said, “that one should sit on Thy right hand, and one on Thy left.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then doth this evangelist say, that their mother came to Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is probable both things were done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, that they took their mother with them, with the purpose of making their entreaty stronger, and in this way to prevail with Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in proof that this is true, as I say, and the request was rather theirs, and that being ashamed they put forward their mother, mark how Christ directs His words to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But rather let us learn, first, what do they ask, and with what disposition, and whence they were moved to this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence then were they moved to this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They saw themselves honored above the rest, and expected from that they should obtain this request also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what can it be they ask?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear another evangelist plainly declaring this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Because He was nigh,” it is said, “to Jerusalem, and because they thought the kingdom of God should immediately appear,”they asked these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they supposed that this was at the doors, and visible, and that having obtained what they asked, they would undergo none of the painful things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither for its own sake only did they seek it, but as though they would also escape the hardships.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also Christ in the first place leads them off from these thoughts, commanding them to await slaughter and dangers, and the utmost terrors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Are ye able,” saith He, “to drink of the cup that I drink of?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let no man be troubled at the apostles being in such an imperfect state.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not yet was the cross accomplished, not yet the grace of the Spirit given.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou wouldest learn their virtue, notice them after these things, and thou wilt see them superior to every passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For with this object He reveals their deficiencies, that after these things thou mightest know what manner of men they became by grace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That then they were asking, in fact, for nothing spiritual, neither had a thought of the kingdom above, is manifest from hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But let us see also, how they come unto Him, and what they say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “We would,” it is said, “that whatsoever we shall desire of Thee, Thou shouldest do it for us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Christ saith to them, “What would ye?”not being ignorant, but that He may compel them to answer, and lay open the wound, and so apply the medicine.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they out of shame and confusion of face, because under the influence of a human passion they were come to do this, took Him privately apart from the disciples, and asked Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they went before, it is said, so that it might not be observable to them, and so said what they wished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was their desire, as I suppose, because they heard, “Ye shall sit on twelve thrones,” to have the first place of these seats.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that they had an advantage over the others, they knew, but they were afraid of Peter, and say, “Command, that one sit on Thy right hand, one on Thy left;” and they urge Him, saying, “Command.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Showing, that they asked nothing spiritual, neither, if they had known again what they were asking, would they have ventured to ask for so much, He saith, “Ye know not what ye ask,” how great, how marvellous, how surpassing even the powers above.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: After that He adds, “Are ye able to drink of the cup that I shall drink of, and to be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how He straightway drew them off from their suspicion, by framing His discourse from the contrary topics?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For ye, He saith, talk to me of honor and crowns, but I to you of conflicts and labors.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is not the season for rewards, neither shall that glory of mine appear now, but the present time is one of slaughter, and wars, and dangers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And see how by the form of His question, He both urges and attracts them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not, “Are ye able to be slain?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Are ye able to pour forth your blood?” but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Are ye able to drink of the cup?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then to attract them to it, He saith, “Which I shall drink of,” that by their fellowship with Him in it they might be made more ready.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And a baptism again calls He it; showing that great was the cleansing the world was to have from the things that were being done.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “They say unto Him, We are able.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Out of their forwardness they straightway undertook it, not knowing even this which they were saying, but looking to hear what they had asked.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Great blessings did He foretell to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: His meaning is, ye shall be counted worthy of martyrdom, and shall suffer these things which I suffer; ye shall close your life by a violent death, and in these things ye shall be partakers with me; “But to sit on my right hand and on my left is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared of my Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Having first elevated their souls, and made them of a higher character, and having rendered them such as sorrow could not subdue, then He reproves their request.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But what can be this present saying?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed there are two points that are subjects of inquiry to many: one, if it be prepared for any to sit on His right hand; and then, if the Lord of all hath not power to bestow it on them for whom it is prepared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is the saying?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If we solve the former point, then the second also will be clear to the inquirers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: No one shall sit on His right hand nor on His left.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that throne is inaccessible to all, I do not say to men only, and saints, and apostles, but even to angels, and archangels, and to all the powers that are on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: At least Paul puts it as a peculiar privilege of the Only-Begotten, saying, “To which of the angels said He at any time, Sit thou on my right hand?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of the angels He saith, who maketh His angels spirits; but unto the Son, ‘Thy throne, O God.’”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then saith He, “To sit on my right hand and on my left is not mine to give,” as though there are some that should sit there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Not as though there are; far from it; but He makes answer to the thoughts of them who ask the favor, condescending to their understanding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither did they know that lofty throne, and His sitting at the right hand of the Father; how should they, when even the things that were much lower than these, and were daily instilled into them, they understood not? but they sought one thing only, to enjoy the first honors, and to stand before the rest, and that no one should stand before them with Him; even as I have already said before, that, since they heard of twelve thrones, in ignorance what the saying could mean, they asked for the first place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What therefore Christ saith is this: “Ye shall die indeed for me, and shall be slain for the sake of the gospel, and shall be partakers with me, as far as regards the passion: but this is not sufficient to secure you the enjoyment of the first seat, and to cause that ye should occupy the first place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if any one else should come, together with the martyrdom, possessed of all the other parts of virtue far more fully than you, not because I love you now, and prefer you to the rest, therefore shall I set aside him that is distinguished by his good works, and give the first honors to you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thus indeed He did not say it, so as not to pain them, but darkly He intimates the self-same thing, saying, “Ye shall drink indeed of my cup, and ye shall be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with; but to sit on my right hand and on my left, this is not mine to give, but it shall be given to those for whom it is prepared.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But for whom is it prepared?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For them who could become distinguished by their works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He said not, It is not mine to give, but my Father’s, lest any should say that He was too weak, or wanting in vigor for their recompense; but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is not mine, but of those for whom it is prepared.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in order that what I say may be more plain, let us work it on an illustration, and let us suppose there was some master of the games, then that many excellent combatants went down to the contest, and that some two of the combatants that were most nearly connected with the master of the games were to come to him and say, “Cause us to be crowned and proclaimed,” confiding in their good-will and friendship with him; and that he were to say to them, “This is not mine to give, but it shall be given to them for whom it is prepared, by their labors, and their toils;” should we indeed condemn him as powerless?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By no means, but we should approve him for his justice, and for having no respect of persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Like then as we should not say that he did not give the crown from want of vigor, but as not wishing to corrupt the law of the games, nor to disturb the order of justice; in like manner now should I say Christ said this, from every motive to compel them, after the grace of God, to set their hopes of salvation and approval on the proof of their own good works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He saith, “For whom it is prepared.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what, saith He, if others should appear better than you?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What, if they should do greater things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For shall ye, because ye have become my disciples, therefore enjoy the first honors, if ye yourselves should not appear worthy of the choice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that He Himself hath power over the whole, is manifest from His having the entire judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to Peter too He speaks thus, “I will give thee the keys of the Heavens.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul also makes this clear where he saith, “Henceforth is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, will give me in that day; and not to me only, but unto all them also which have loved His appearing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the appearing was of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that no one will stand before Paul, is surely clear to every one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if He hath expressed these things somewhat obscurely, marvel not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to lead them on by hidden instruction,not to be rudely pressing Him without object or cause for the first honors (for from a human passion they felt this), and not wishing to give them pain, by the obscurity He effects both these objects.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Then were the ten moved with indignation with respect to the two.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When He had reproved them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So long as the judgment was Christ’s, they were not moved with indignation; but seeing them preferred, they were contented, and held their peace, out of reverence and honor to their Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if they were vexed in mind, yet they dared not utter this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And when they had some feeling of human weakness towards Peter, at the time that He gave the didrachmas, they did not give way to anger, but asked only, “Who then is greatest?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But since here the request was the disciples’, they are moved with indignation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even here are they straightway moved with indignation, when they asked, but when Christ had reproved them, and had said they should not enjoy the first honors, unless they showed themselves worthy of these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how they were all in an imperfect state, when both these were lifting themselves up above the ten, and those envying the two?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But, as I said, show me them after these things, and thou wilt see them delivered from all these passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear at least how this same John, he who now came to Him for these things, everywhere gives up the first place to Peter, both in addressing the people, and in working miracles, in the Acts of the Apostles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And he conceals not Peter’s good deeds, but relates both the confession, which he openly made when all were silent,and his entering into the tomb,and puts the apostle before himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, because both continued with Him at His crucifixion, taking away the ground of his own commendation, he saith, “That disciple was known unto the high priest.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But James survived not a long time, but from the beginning he was so greatly filled with warmth, and so forsook all the things of men, and mounted up to an height unutterable, as straightway to be slain.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, in all respects, they after these things became excellent.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But then, “they were moved with indignation.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He called them unto Him, and said, The princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, as they were disturbed and troubled, He soothes them by His call before His word, and by drawing them near Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the two having separated themselves from the company of the ten, had stood nearer Him, pleading their own interests.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He brings near Him these also, by this very act, and by exposing and revealing it before the rest, soothing the passion both of the one and of the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not as before, so now also doth He check them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas before He brings little children into the midst, and commands to imitate their simplicity and lowliness; here He reproves them in a sharper way from the contrary side, saying, “The princes of the Gentiles exercise dominionover them, and their great ones exercise authority upon them, but it shall not be so among you;but he that will be great among you, let this man be minister to all; and he that will be first, let him be last of all;”showing that such a feeling as this is that of heathens, I mean, to love the first place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the passion is tyrannical, and is continually hindering even great men; therefore also it needs a severer stripe.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Whence He too strikes deeper into them, by comparison with the Gentiles shaming their inflamed soul, and removes the envy of the one and the arrogance of the other, all but saying, “Be not moved with indignation, as insulted.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they harm and disgrace themselves most, who on this wise seek the first places, for they are amongst the last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For matters with us are not like matters without.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: ‘For the princes of the Gentiles exercise dominion over them,’ but with me the last, even he is first.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And in proof that I say not these things without cause, by the things which I do and suffer, receive the proof of my sayings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I have myself done something even more.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For being King of the powers above, I was willing to become man, and I submitted to be despised, and despitefully entreated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even with these things was I satisfied, but even unto death did I come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore,” He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Even as the Son of Man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister, and to give His life a ransom for many.”“For not even at this did I stop,” saith He, “but even my life did I give a ransom; and for whom?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For enemies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But thou if thou art abused, it is for thyself, but I for thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not then afraid, as though thine honor were plucked down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For how much soever thou humblest thyself, thou canst not descend so much as thy Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet His descent hath become the ascent of all, and hath made His own glory shine forth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For before He was made man, He was known amongst angels only; but after He was made man and was crucified, so far from lessening that glory, He acquired other besides, even that from the knowledge of the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Fear not then, as though thine honor were put down, if thou shouldest abase thyself, for in this way is thy glory more exalted, in this way it becomes greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This is the door of the kingdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then go the opposite way, neither let us war against ourselves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if we desire to appear great, we shall not be great, but even the most dishonored of all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how everywhere He urges them by the opposite things, giving them what they desire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in the preceding parts also we have shown this in many instances, and in the cases of the covetous, and of the vain-glorious, He did thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For wherefore, He saith, dost thou give alms before men?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That thou mayest enjoy glory?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou must then not do so, and thou shalt surely enjoy it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore dost thou lay up treasures?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That thou mayest be rich?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thou must then not lay up treasures, and thou shalt be rich.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even so here too, wherefore dost thou set thy heart on the first places?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That thou mayest be before others?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Choose then the last place, and then thou wilt enjoy the first.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So that if it be thy will to become great, seek not to become great, and then thou wilt be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the other is to be little.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He drew them off from the disease, by showing them both from thence failing of their object, and from hence gaining, that they might flee the one, and follow after the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And of the Gentiles, too, He for this cause reminded them, that in this way again He might show the thing to be disgraceful and to be abhorred.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the arrogant is of necessity base, and, on the contrary, the lowly-minded is high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this is the height that is true and genuine, and exists not in name only, nor in manner of address.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that which is from without is of necessity and fear, but this is like to God’s.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Such a one, though he be admired by no one, continues high; even as again the other, though he be courted by all, is of all men the basest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the one is an honor rendered of necessity, whence also it easily passes away; but the other is of principle, whence also it continues steadfast.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since for this we admire the saints also, that being greater than all, they humbled themselves more than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore even to this day they continue to be high, and not even death hath brought down that height.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if ye be minded, let us by reasonings also inquire into this very thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Any one is said to be high, either when he is so by greatness of stature, or when he hath chanced to be set on a high place, and low in like manner, from the opposite things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us see then who is like this, the boaster, or he that keeps within measure, that thou mayest perceive that nothing is higher than lowliness of mind, and nothing lower than boastfulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The boaster then desires to be greater than all, and affirms no one to be equal in worth with him; and how much soever honor he may obtain, he sets his heart on more and claims it, and accounts himself to have obtained none, and treats men with utter contempt, and yet seeks after the honor that comes from them; than which what can be more unreasonable?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this surely is like an enigma.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By those, whom he holds in no esteem, he desires to be glorified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how he who desires to be exalted falls down and is set on the ground?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that he accounts all men to be nothing compared with himself, he himself declares, for this is boasting.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then dost cast thyself upon him who is nothing? why dost thou seek honor of him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou lead about with thee such great multitudes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou one low, and set on a low place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Come then, let us inquire about the high man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This one knows what man is, and that man is a great thing, and that he himself is last of all, and therefore whatever honor he may enjoy, he reckons this great, so that this one is consistent with himself and is high, and shifts not his judgment; for whom he accounts great, the honors that come from them he esteems great also, though they should chance to be small, because he accounts those who bestow them to be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the boastful man accounts them that give the honors to be nothing, yet the honors bestowed by them he reckons to be great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, the lowly man is seized by no passion, no anger can much trouble this man, no love of glory, no envy, no jealousy: and what can be higher than the soul that is delivered from these things?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the boastful man is held in subjection by all these things, like any worm crawling in the mire, for jealousy and envy and anger are forever troubling his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which then is high?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that is superior to his passions, or he that is their slave?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He that trembles at them and is afraid of them, or he that is unsubdued, and never taken by them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which kind of bird should we say flies higher? that which is higher than the hands and the arrows of the hunter, or that which does not even suffer the hunters to need an arrow, from his flying along the ground, and from not being able ever to elevate himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is not then the arrogant man like this? for indeed every net readily catches him as crawling on the ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou wilt, even from that wicked demon prove thou this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For what can be baser than the devil, because he had exalted himself; what higher than the man who is willing to abase himself?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For the former crawls on the ground under our heel (For, “ye tread,” He saith,“upon serpents and scorpions”), but the latter is set with the angels on high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou desirest to learn this from the example of haughty men also, consider that barbarian king, that led so great an army, who knew not so much as the things that are manifest to all; as, for instance, that stone was stone, and the images, images; wherefore he was inferior even to these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But the godly and faithful are raised even above the sun; than whom what can be higher, who rise above even the vaults of heaven, and passing beyond angels, stand by the very throne of the king.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And that thou mayest learn in another way their vileness; who will be abased?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He who has God for his ally, or he with whom God is at war?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is quite plain that it is he with whom He is at war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear then touching either of these what saith the Scripture.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “God resisteth the proud, but giveth grace unto the humble.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, I will ask you another thing also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which is higher?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He who acts as a priest to God and offers sacrifice? or he who is somewhere far removed from confidence towards Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what manner of sacrifice doth the lowly man offer? one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Hear David saying, “The sacrifice of God is a contrite spirit; a contrite and humbled heart God will not despise.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou the purity of this man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Behold also the uncleanness of the other; for “every one that is proud in heart is unclean before God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Besides, the one hath God resting upon him, (“For unto whom will I look,” saith He, “but to him that is meek and quiet, and trembleth at my words”),but the other crawls with the devil, for he that is lifted up with pride shall suffer the devil’s punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore Paul also said, “Lest, being lifted up with pride, he should fall into the condemnation of the devil.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the thing opposite to what he wishes, befalls him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For his wish is to be arrogant, that he may be honored; but the most contemned of all is this character.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For these most of all are laughing stocks, foes and enemies to all men, the most easy to be subdued by their enemies, the men that easily fall into anger, the unclean before God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then can be worse than this, for this is the extremity of evils?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is sweeter than the lowly, what more blessed, since they are longed after, and beloved of God?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the glory too that cometh of men, these do most of all enjoy, and all honor them as fathers, embrace them as brothers, receive them as their own members.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us then become lowly, that we may be high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For most utterly doth arrogance abase.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This abased Pharaoh.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “I know not,” he saith, “the Lord,”and he became inferior to flies and frogs, and the locusts, and after that with his very arms and horses was he drowned in the sea.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In direct opposition to him, Abraham saith, “I am dust and ashes,”and prevailed over countless barbarians, and having fallen into the midst of Egyptians, returned, bearing a trophy more glorious than the former, and, cleaving to this virtue, grew ever more high.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore he is celebrated everywhere, therefore he is crowned and proclaimed; but Pharaoh is both earth and ashes, and if there is anything else more vile than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing doth God so abhor as arrogance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this object hath He done all things from the beginning, in order that He might root out this passion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because of this are we become mortal, and are in sorrows, and wailings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because of this are we in toil, and sweat, and in labor continual, and mingled with affliction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed out of arrogance did the first man sin, looking for an equality with God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, not even what things he had, did he continue to possess, but lost even these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For arrogance is like this, so far from adding to us any improvement of our life, it subtracts even what we have; as, on the contrary, humility, so far from subtracting from what we have, adds to us also what we have not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This virtue then let us emulate, this let us pursue, that we may both enjoy present honor, and attain unto the glory to come, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, with whom be unto the Father glory and might, together with the Holy Ghost, now and always, and world without end.
